user_query:
I need a focused weekly money review using my CSV exports. Please read these files under input/:
- input/transactions.csv (normalized per schema: date, amount, merchant, category, account, note)
- input/balances.csv (columns: account,balance,currency,as_of_date)
- input/debts.csv (columns: name,balance,apr_percent,min_payment,due_day,notes)
Assume today is 2026-03-12.

Deliverables (write all outputs under output/):
1) output/cashflow_summary.json — Compute and round to 2 decimals:
   - total_inflow, total_outflow, net_cashflow, avg_daily_burn
   - monthly_totals: object keyed by YYYY-MM with {in, out, net}
   - top_categories: array of 5 items [{name, spend}]
   - top_merchants: array of 5 items [{name, spend}]
2) output/recurring.json — Array of recurring charge candidates detected from transactions (spend only). Each item: {merchant, cadence, count, average_amount}. Cadence should be "monthly" if gaps are ~30 days and amounts are similar.
3) output/runway_review.json — A JSON runway snapshot for the next 14 days (2026-03-12 to 2026-03-26), with fields:
   {"date_assumed":"2026-03-12","cash_today":<sum of balances.csv>,"obligations_next_14_days":[{"name":"...","amount":...,"due_date":"YYYY-MM-DD"}],"total_obligations_next_14_days":...,"free_to_spend":...}
   - Derive upcoming obligations from detected recurring bills and the debts file: project the next due date for each monthly merchant based on the last observed date, and use due_day from debts.csv to compute the next due date this month. Round amounts to 2 decimals.
4) output/debt_triage.json — Array ordered by priority (1 = highest) using near-term damage, rate, and monthly pressure. Include for each: {"name","due_date","apr_percent","min_payment","priority"}.
5) output/next_actions.md — 3 concrete actions (pay/cancel/renegotiate/monitor) tied to the upcoming due dates (include exact due dates in the text).

Do not include any personal identifiers beyond what’s in the files. Guidance is informational only, not financial advice.
